vci_watchlist.name: AbCellera Biologics
vci_watchlist.nvidia_signals: 6/6 NVIDIA-CLASS | S1 PRESENT (104 partner programs; Lilly FDA-approved drug on platform) | S2 PRESENT (Bruker forced to license; IP-protected B-cell microfluidics) | S3 PRESENT (partners fund multi-year programs with royalty stakes) | S4 PRESENT (R&D 200-600% of revenue sustained 3+ years) | S5 PRESENT (royalty model across 104 programs; NK3R across CNS/metabolic/reproductive) | S6 PRESENT (Hansen founder; $740K personal buy at $2.61; 90 papers; 90+ patents) | S5-MULTIPLIER: NOT active
vci_watchlist.status: CATALYST ALERT — hold for Phase 2 ABCL635 efficacy readout Q3 2026. Entry $4.50-5.25 on pullback pre-data OR at market on Phase 2 positive. No entry above $5.25 pre-data. Size 1.0% initial; scale to 1.5% if Phase 2 positive
vci_watchlist.thesis_break_summary: T1: pharma partners develop in-house antibody discovery at comparable throughput and abandon ABCL platform; T2: Hansen departs and replacement explicitly deprioritises platform R&D investment; T3: two or more of Lilly/AbbVie/Biogen/Regeneron simultaneously terminate multi-year strategic programs; T4: partner program count declines >20% in one year without strategic consolidation explanation; T5: competing AI antibody discovery platform achieves demonstrated parity in drug approval rate at production scale; T6: Health Canada or FDA structural removal; T7: mRNA or gene therapy paradigm eliminates antibody drug modality as therapeutic class
vci_watchlist.rescore_catalyst: Phase 2 ABCL635 efficacy readout Q3 2026 — enter at market if positive; re-score post-binary event
vci_watchlist.phase2_status: First patients dosed Jan 2026. Multicenter, randomised, double-blind, 80 postmenopausal women. Q3 2026 efficacy readout = binary
vci_watchlist.acs_breakdown: ACS1=10 ACS2=10 ACS3=7 ACS4=11 ACS5=10 ACS6=7 ACS7=10 ACS8=8 ACS9=5 | Total=78

vci_watchlist.name: Astera Labs
vci_watchlist.nvidia_signals: 4/6 | S1 PRESENT | S2 PRESENT | S3 PRESENT | S4 PRESENT | S5 UNCERTAIN | S6 UNCERTAIN
vci_watchlist.status: WATCHLIST — entry level $160-170; current price ~$306 (far from trigger). Monitor for pullback
vci_watchlist.thesis_break_summary: T1: PCIe/CXL interconnect standard abandoned by hyperscalers in favour of proprietary fabric; T2: management vision shift away from open-standard connectivity platform; T3: two or more hyperscaler customers publicly commit to competing proprietary solution; T5: competing connectivity platform achieves production-scale hyperscaler adoption displacing PCIe/CXL layer
vci_watchlist.rescore_catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings — revenue/margin beat; analyst target refresh
vci_watchlist.acs_breakdown: No change from May 2026 run | ACS 65

vci_watchlist.name: Credo Technology
vci_watchlist.nvidia_signals: 3/6 | S1 PRESENT | S2 PRESENT | S3 PRESENT | S4 UNCERTAIN | S5 UNCERTAIN | S6 UNCERTAIN
vci_watchlist.status: WATCHLIST — entry level $140-150; current price ~$218 (far from trigger). Monitor for pullback
vci_watchlist.thesis_break_summary: T1: active electrical cable technology displaced by competing optical/photonic solution at hyperscaler production scale; T2: management abandons high-speed connectivity focus; T3: two or more hyperscaler customers terminate AEC programmes; T5: competing AEC or optics platform achieves demonstrated parity at lower cost in production deployment
vci_watchlist.acs_breakdown: No change from May 2026 run | ACS 63

vci_watchlist.name: Recursion Pharmaceuticals
vci_watchlist.nvidia_signals: 4/6 (S5-MULTIPLIER active) | S1 PRESENT | S2 PRESENT | S3 PRESENT | S4 PRESENT | S5 PRESENT (S5-MULT) | S6 UNCERTAIN (NVIDIA equity exit Feb 2026; Gibson off board Jun 2026)
vci_watchlist.classification: PIPELINE — floor-capped (B1+B2=3/6; raw ACS 63 capped at 60)
vci_watchlist.status: FLOOR-CAPPED — no entry until September 2026 re-score confirms ACS above floor. Price at trigger $3.01 but re-score required. Entry range $3.00-3.50 subject to re-score
vci_watchlist.floor_reason: B1+B2=3/6 (platform ecosystem thin; B1=1 pharma bilateral, no developer SDK; B2=2 sticky but Amazon Bio Discovery creates substitution risk). Raw ACS 63 capped at 60.
vci_watchlist.thesis_break_summary: T1: Amazon Bio Discovery or competing AI platform achieves demonstrated parity in drug candidate generation with pharma adoption at RXRX partner scale; T2: Khan (replacement CEO) explicitly deprioritises AI-biology OS strategy; T3: two or more top-5 partners (Roche, Bayer, Sanofi) simultaneously terminate strategic programs; T5: competing AI drug discovery platform achieves production-scale pharma adoption displacing RXRX OS layer; T6: FDA or SEC structural enforcement
vci_watchlist.rescore_catalyst: REC-1245 DAHLIA 2H26 dose escalation data; Roche partnership renewal signals; Amazon Bio Discovery competitive traction assessment
vci_watchlist.acs_breakdown: ACS1=5 ACS2=10 ACS3=5 ACS4=6 ACS5=7 ACS6=10 ACS7=5 ACS8=10 ACS9=5 | Raw=63 → Capped at 60 (B1+B2=3/6 floor)

_vci_watchlist_meta.description: VCI (asymmetric) candidates promoted from project_isa_vci_watchlist.md. Populated at the end of each VCI run. fetch_watchlist_metrics.py fetches current price for in-window check but does NOT re-score via yfinance — ACS score is stored here from the VCI pipeline. score_partab.py uses the VCI formatting path for these entries.

example_entry.name: Example Corp
example_entry.thesis_break_summary: T1: revenue growth below 20% for 2 consecutive quarters

stock_sleeve.name: Micron Technology
stock_sleeve.note: +65% gain — reassess trim vs hold at June run

stock_sleeve.name: Oxford Nanopore Technologies PLC
stock_sleeve.note: Auto-added from broker file 06-Jun-2026 — verify purchase_date / cost basis / thesis-break conditions

_update_instructions: After each monthly review run, Claude updates: (1) watchlist entries — adjust ranks, entry_levels, statuses, add/remove names; always include source_pipeline on every entry ('growth_stock' or 'energy') and path ('A' or 'C'); score_history and normalised_score are maintained by update_watchlist.py at pre-run — Claude does NOT manually update these; (2) stock_sleeve — add new purchases (set purchase_date + cost_per_share + source_pipeline), remove sold positions; (3) vci_watchlist — populated from project_isa_vci_watchlist.md at end of each VCI run; max 5 entries; set source_pipeline='vci', acs_score, vci_run_date, nvidia_signals, classification; (4) last_updated date. Keep in sync with project_isa_watchlist.md and project_isa_trades_log.md. ENERGY candidates promoted to watchlist from energy_screener use source_pipeline='energy' and path='C'. update_watchlist.py owns promotion/ranking logic and all score_history/normalised_score updates.
